content.principles: 1. Webpack: Bundler-based. Builds a complete dependency graph and bundles all modules before serving. Cold start is slow for large apps. 2. Vite: ESM-based. Uses native ES Modules in development, serving modules on demand via browser requests. Uses Esbuild (Go-based) for pre-bundling dependencies. 3. Production: Both use bundling (Webpack's own vs Vite's Rollup) for optimized assets.
pitfalls: Vite's slow initial page load when there are hundreds of small ESM requests.
Compatibility issues when using non-ESM-compliant legacy libraries in Vite.
Complex Webpack configurations becoming 'black boxes' that are hard to maintain.
